meta.name: Lentil Soup
meta.subtitle: Budget-friendly • high-protein vegetarian baseline • purslane optional

source.notes: Designed for SSA meal planning and batch cooking. Flexible veg, pantry-friendly, freezer-stable. Purslane option included for nutrition/texture.

yield.notes: Makes ~10–12 cups depending on simmer and thickness.

tasteProfile.notes: Savory lentils with tomato and warm spices; brightened with lemon and herbs.

ingredients.label: Brown or green lentils, rinsed
ingredients.notes: About 1 1/2 cups dry. Holds shape well.

ingredients.label: Vegetable broth (or water + bouillon), low-sodium preferred
ingredients.notes: About 7 cups. Use water if needed; season at the end.

ingredients.label: Water (optional, adjust thickness)
ingredients.notes: Add during simmer if soup thickens too much.

ingredients.label: Black pepper
ingredients.notes: About 1/2 teaspoon, plus more to taste.

ingredients.label: Salt (adjust to broth)
ingredients.notes: Start with ~1 teaspoon if using low-sodium broth; adjust at end.

ingredients.label: Lemon juice (optional)
ingredients.notes: 1–2 tablespoons to taste. Brightens the soup.

ingredients.label: Purslane, roughly chopped (optional)
ingredients.notes: Stir in at the end like greens. Adds a pleasant tang and succulent texture.

ingredients.label: Spinach or other leafy greens, chopped (optional)

steps.title: Sweat aromatics
instructions: Heat oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add onion, carrots, and celery. Cook 6–8 minutes until softened.
Add garlic and cook 30 seconds until fragrant.

steps.title: Bloom spices and build base
instructions: Stir in cumin (and smoked paprika/thyme if using) for 20–30 seconds to bloom.
Add tomato paste (if using) and cook 1 minute.
Add diced tomatoes and stir, scraping any fond from the pot.

steps.title: Add lentils and simmer
instructions: Add rinsed lentils, broth, bay leaf, black pepper, and an initial pinch of salt.
Bring to a gentle simmer, then reduce heat to maintain a steady simmer.
Simmer 30–40 minutes until lentils are tender (time depends on lentil age).

steps.title: Adjust texture (optional) and season
instructions: For a thicker soup, use an immersion blender to blend 1–2 cups of soup in the pot (or mash some lentils with a spoon), then stir.
Taste and adjust salt and pepper. Add water/broth if too thick.

steps.title: Finish with brightness and greens
instructions: Turn off heat. Stir in lemon juice (if using) and parsley (if using).
If using purslane or spinach, stir in and let wilt 1–2 minutes from residual heat.

pairings.notes: If your schema rejects extra pairing fields, keep this object minimal.

bestPractices: This soup freezes very well. Cool quickly and portion.
If adding leafy greens, consider adding them fresh when reheating for best texture.

useWhatYouHaveLogic.notes: If no tomatoes: omit and add 10g vinegar at end for brightness. If no celery: substitute diced bell pepper or extra carrot/onion.

storage.fridge: Refrigerate up to 5 days. Thickens as it sits; loosen with water/broth.
storage.freezer: Freeze up to 3 months. Thaw overnight and reheat gently; add water/broth if needed.
storage.reheat: Reheat to a simmer; adjust salt and add lemon/greens fresh if desired.

notes: Salt late if using water-only broth; lentils absorb salt as they cook.
If lentils are old, they may take longer to soften—extend simmer and add water as needed.
Purslane adds a unique tang and succulent bite; add at the end to preserve texture.

audit.testNotes: Fixed JSON syntax and removed disallowed pairings.toppings field. Schema-compliant: schemaVersion+meta present; ingredients include id+label+amount; steps include id+title+instructions.
